data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the issues. In MSTest 4.1.0:

1. **`TestMethodAttribute.Execute(ITestMethod)` was removed** — The `Execute` method is no longer virtual/overridable on `TestMethodAttribute`. The class is now sealed or the method was removed. This means the `sealed class TimedTestMethodAttribute : TestMethodAttribute` with `override Execute` doesn't work.

2. **`TestTimeout.Infinite` was removed** — The `TestTimeout` class/enum no longer exists.

Let me research what the MSTest 4.1.0 migration path is for these:

1. For custom `TestMethodAttribute` with `Execute` override: In MSTest v3+, the recommended approach is to use `ITestDataSource` or other extensibility points. 

In MSTest 4.x, the `Execute` method was completely removed from `TestMethodAttribute`, so I need to find what the new extensibility model uses instead. Let me check what APIs are actually available in the MSTest 4.1.0 package to understand the replacement pattern.

For the timeout behavior, `TestTimeout.Infinite` was likely replaced with something like `Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan` or the attribute now accepts different values. Looking at the MSTest migration path, it seems `TestMethodAttribute` is now sealed and custom test execution should use the `ITestMethodDecorator` interface pattern instead.

<member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute">
            This attribute doesn't currently provide any different functionality compared to <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>. It's only
            present for backward compatibility. Using <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/> is recommended, even for parameterized tests.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)">
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute"/> class.
            Multiple methods with this attribute in the assembly is allowed, but there is no guarantee of the order in which they will be executed.
            The test cleanup attribute marks methods that are executed after every test marked with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>.
            Each thread of execution will be handed a TestClass worth of tests to execute.
            Within the TestClass, the test methods will execute serially.
            Each thread of execution will be handed TestMethods to execute.
            Multiple methods with this attribute in the assembly is allowed, but there is no guarantee of the order in which they will be executed. In addition, TimeoutAttribute isn't supported on methods with this attribute.
            Note that before RetryAttribute is considered, the test was already executed once.

1. **`Execute` → `ExecuteAsync`**: In MSTest 4.1.0, `TestMethodAttribute` no longer has the synchronous `Execute(ITestMethod)` virtual method. Instead, it has `ExecuteAsync(ITestMethod)` which returns `Task<TestResult[]>`. Extensions should override `ExecuteAsync` instead of `Execute`.

2. **`TestTimeout.Infinite` removed**: The `TestTimeout` enum/class no longer exists. Since `TimeoutAttribute` takes an `int` (milliseconds), for infinite timeout we should use `System.Threading.Timeout.Infinite` (which is the constant `-1` from `System.Threading`).


3. **`TestMethodAttribute` is not sealed**, so subclasses like `STATestMethodAttribute` can inherit from it. I need to update the override signature from the synchronous `Execute` method to the async `ExecuteAsync` method, replace the base call with an awaited async version, swap out `TestTimeout.Infinite` for `System.Threading.Timeout.Infinite`, and add the necessary `using System.Threading` directive.

**Build succeeds with 0 errors.** Here's what changed for MSTest 4.1.0:

| Breaking change | Fix |
|---|---|
| `Execute(ITestMethod)` removed | Override `ExecuteAsync(ITestMethod)` instead (returns `Task<TestResult[]>`) |
| `TestTimeout.Infinite` removed | Use `System.Threading.Timeout.Infinite` (add `using System.Threading;`) |

The remaining warnings (MSTEST0032, MSTEST0057) are just analyzer advisories about your placeholder `Assert.IsTrue(true)` calls and an optional `[CallerFilePath]` parameter on the custom attribute constructor—neither blocks compilation or test execution.
